prompt: The custom functional interface does not compile. What is the bug?
choices: A functional interface must have exactly one abstract method; Transformer declares two abstract methods (transform and reverse), violating the @FunctionalInterface contract — the compiler rejects it
Interfaces cannot declare methods that return String
reverse is a reserved method name in Java interfaces
explanation: @FunctionalInterface requires exactly one abstract method so it can be implemented by a lambda or method reference. Transformer has two abstract methods (transform and reverse), so the annotation causes a compile error. Fix: remove reverse, or make it a default method with a body.
